'''Treva Horme''' was a [[Lutrillian/Legends|Lutrillian]] [[Gender/Legends|Female]] and the primary saleswoman and executive planner for [[Planet Dreams, Incorporated]]. Her duties included accounting and the monitoring of production schedules.<ref name="GG3" />

==Biography==
The hard-working Horme was an equal partner in [[Planet Dreams, Incorporated]] along with the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[Wiorkettle]] and [[Ozz]] the [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]].<ref name="GG3" /> Much of her notoriety came from her work with [[Outer Javin Company]], where clients included [[Tagge Company/Legends|TaggeCo]] and [[Lynciro Corporation]].<ref name="Rough">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040325adv|text=Rough and Tundra|int=Rough and Tundra}}</ref> In approximately [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], the company's head offices were relocated to [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin's]] [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]].<ref name="GG3" />

[[File:Treva-Horme-tesb.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Horme running past [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] during the evacuation of Cloud City]]
The three Planet Dreams executives were considering selling private hovering platforms in Bespin's [[Life Zone/Legends|Life Zone]] when Ozz revealed that someone was embezzling money and terraforming equipment from the company. Ozz vowed to investigate but was interrupted in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] arrived and [[Occupation of Bespin|took control]] of the city. The partners decided to download all of their files and evacuate, but while Horme and Wiorkettle followed the plan, Ozz disappeared. The remaining partners moved back to the [[Oseon asteroid belt]] after fleeing Bespin.<ref name="GG3" />

In addition to [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and her own [[Lutrillian (language)|native language]], Horme was able to speak [[Rodian (language)|Rodian]].<ref name="Rough" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Treva_Horme_and_Wiorkettle_TESB.jpg|250px|thumb|Horme and Wiorkettle on Cloud City]]
Treva Horme was a background character played by actor [[Steven Meek|Steve Meek]]<ref name="StevenMeeksAutograph">{{Facebook|username=StarwarsAutographCollecting|url=photos/a.234794036554094/1392210544145765|date=25-10-2016|archivedate=20211011200349}}</ref> in {{Film|V}}, but can barely be seen in the film beyond a blurred figure passing by.<ref name="TESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> The character was most prominently seen in behind the scenes images and promotional photos for the film, in every case paired with another background character, Wiorkettle. Artwork from a [[1980]] [[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back Coloring Book (Cloud City)|coloring book]] based on the film is patterned after one of these promotional photos<ref name="Coloring">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back Coloring Book (Cloud City)|''Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back Coloring Book'' (Cloud City)]]</ref>, while Horme appears in the foreground of a panel from [[Marvel Comics]]' adaptation of the film.<ref name="Marvel 43">[[Star Wars (1977) 43|''Star Wars'' (1977) 43]]</ref>

Horme and Wiorkettle did not receive names until the second edition of [[West End Games]]' [[1996]] [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="GG3" /> The names were [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively applied]] to the film characters in the ''[[Cloud City Limited]]'' set of the ''[[Star Wars Customizable Card Game]]'' in [[1997]].<ref name="CCG" />
